source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Arena Offshore East Cameron 328 B

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Pressurized Oil Release
event.incident_type: Pollution event with thermal burn injury
actions_taken: Manual actuation of emergency shut-down station
Installation of one inch nipple and ball valve to isolate leak
Positioning of plywood sheet to direct oil flow into containment skid
National Response Center Incident Report filed (# 1086799)
event.summary: On June 23, 2014, at approximately 10:30 A.M., the Person in Charge (PIC) at Arena Offshore East Cameron 328 B facility was replacing a faulty pneumatic temperature controller (TC) on Heater Treater #2 (NBK-2770). The TC was installed directly into a thread-o-let within the liquid medium of the vessel instead of inside a thermo-well as recommended by the manufacturer. Prior to removal, the vessel was not depressurized and hot oil was not drained to a safe level. When the TC was removed, hot pressurized oil blew out of the one inch opening, striking and burning the PIC on his left bicep. Oil continued to flow across the deck, striking the line heater approximately 20 feet away. Operations personnel manually shut down the facility and installed a nipple and ball valve to isolate the leak. A total of 12 gallons of crude oil was discharged into offshore waters.
recommendations: Ensure temperature controllers are installed inside thermo-wells as recommended by manufacturer
Implement depressurization procedures prior to removing pressurized vessel components
Drain hot oil to safe levels below component removal points
Allow sufficient cooling time before maintenance activities
Enhance Job Safety Analysis (JSA) to identify hazards specific to non-standard equipment installations

hazards.name: Pressurized hot oil in heater treater vessel
Non-standard temperature controller installation (direct thread-o-let vs. thermo-well)
High temperature liquid medium in vessel

threats.name: Failure to depressurize vessel prior to component removal
Failure to drain hot oil to safe level below temperature controller
Inadequate Job Safety Analysis identifying non-standard equipment hazards
Insufficient cooling time before maintenance activity

consequences.name: Loss of containment - crude oil discharge to environment
Uncontrolled oil flow across facility deck

controls.name: Depressurization of vessel prior to component removal
controls.barrier_role: Critical mitigation to prevent pressurized release
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to eliminate pressure hazard before opening vessel

supporting_text: the vessel was not depressurized and the hot oil was not drained to a safe level below the TC
critical mitigations were not executed prior to removing the TC from the vessel

controls.name: Draining hot oil to safe level below temperature controller
controls.barrier_role: Critical mitigation to reduce liquid level and thermal hazard
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to eliminate liquid release hazard at removal point

supporting_text: the hot oil was not drained to a safe level below the TC
critical mitigations were not executed prior to removing the TC from the vessel

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) identifying hazards specific to non-standard equipment installation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify and plan for hazards
controls.lod_basis: Planning control to ensure hazards are recognized and mitigations planned

supporting_text: the JSA for this task identified hazards, including: pressure, temperature, and liquid release; however, the JSA failed to specifically identify the primary potential threat which was the TC not being installed inside a thermo-well

Operations personnel failed to recognize the potential hazards associated with the TC being installed directly into the vessel instead of inside a thermo-well; therefore adequate mitigations were not identified on the JSA

controls.name: Allowing sufficient cooling time before maintenance activity
controls.barrier_role: Critical mitigation to reduce thermal hazard
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to reduce temperature of liquid medium before opening vessel

supporting_text: critical mitigations were not executed prior to removing the TC from the vessel which jeopardized the safety of personnel
These critical mitigations included: depressurizing the vessel, lowering the liquid levels within the vessel, and allowing sufficient time for cooling

controls.name: Manual emergency shut-down station actuation
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response to stop fluid inflow and isolate hazard
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to stop flow after loss of containment detected

supporting_text: Operations personnel then shut-in the rest of the facility by manually actuating an emergency shut-down station

controls.name: Installation of isolation valve (nipple and ball valve) at leak point
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to isolate leak source after detection

supporting_text: Once the pressurized flow of oil declined, operations personnel installed a one inch nipple and ball valve into the open thread-o-let, isolating the leak

controls.name: Containment skid beneath heater treater vessel
controls.barrier_role: Secondary containment to capture spilled oil
controls.lod_basis: Tertiary control to contain environmental release

supporting_text: In an effort to direct the oil into the containment skid beneath the vessel, operations personnel positioned a sheet of plywood in front of the oil stream

controls.name: Isolation of vessel liquid dump lines prior to maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Precautionary measure to prevent unintended fluid release

supporting_text: operations personnel took the following precautionary measures in preparation for replacement of the TC: manually blocked the boarding valve on the Vermillion 342 A incoming pipeline, isolated the vessel's liquid dump lines, and isolated the make-up gas to the vessel

controls.name: Isolation of make-up gas to vessel prior to maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Precautionary measure to prevent pressure buildup

supporting_text: operations personnel took the following precautionary measures in preparation for replacement of the TC: manually blocked the boarding valve on the Vermillion 342 A incoming pipeline, isolated the vessel's liquid dump lines, and isolated the make-up gas to the vessel

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
